multiplechoiceresponse.label: A hiring dataset is defined to be α-biased if the difference in job-offer rates between Group A and Group B is at least α = 0.05. The dataset shows that Group A receives offers 48% of the time and Group B receives offers 46% of the time. Which statement is most accurate?

choicegroup.choice: The dataset is α-biased because the rates are not exactly equal.
The dataset is not α-biased because the difference (0.02) is smaller than α = 0.05
The dataset is α-biased because hiring decisions involve human choices.
The dataset becomes α-unbiased if we train logistic regression instead of another model.

div.p: A dataset is defined to be α‑biased when the difference in positive outcome rates between two groups is at least α. Here the difference in positive outcome rates is 0.48 − 0.46 = 0.02. Because 0.02 < 0.05, the dataset does not meet the lecture’s criterion for α-bias at this threshold. Human decision-making can create bias, but α-bias is still a quantitative condition determined by the measured outcome-rate gap.
